title: An Italian Song.
content:
Dear is my little native vale,
    The ring-dove builds and murmurs there;
    Close by my cot she tells her tale
    To every passing villager.
    The squirrel leaps from tree to tree,
    And shells his nuts at liberty.

    In orange-groves and myrtle-bowers,
    That breathe a gale of fragrance round,
    I charm the fairy-footed hours
    With my lov'd lute's romantic sound;
    Or crowns of living laurel weave,
    For those that win the race at eve.

    The shepherd's horn at break of day,
    The ballet danc'd in twilight glade,
    The canzonet and roundelay
    Sung in the silent green-wood shade;
    These simple joys, that never fail,
    Shall bind me to my native vale.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Samuel Rogers delivers a powerful performance in "An Italian Song."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
